title: Saurauia scabra
what_description: Saurauia scabra is a species of plant in the family Actinidiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant is an evergreen shrub or small tree, growing up to 10 meters in height, with a broad, rounded crown and a straight, cylindrical trunk. Its leaves are simple, alternate, and elliptical in shape, measuring 10-20 cm in length, with a short petiole and a serrated margin. The flowers are white to pale yellow in color, solitary or in small clusters, and measure 1-2 cm in diameter, with a funnel-shaped calyx and a corolla with five lobes. The fruit is a yellowish-red berry, measuring 2-4 cm in diameter, with a leathery skin and several seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Saurauia scabra are inflammation, fever, and diarrhea. Rheumatism symptoms are alleviated by Saurauia scabra, which has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, Saurauia scabra has been found to reduce asthma symptoms, gastroenteritis, malaria, diabetes, urinary tract infections, skin irritation, arthritis, and respiratory issues, while also having potential anti-cancer properties and being effective against digestive issues, dyspepsia, and skin infections.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Saurauia scabra are antioxidant, which may be attributed to its content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. Antimicrobial activities have been reported, particularly against bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of saponins and volatile oils. Its anti-inflammatory properties are thought to be mediated by the plant's saponins and alkaloids, which can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The analgesic properties of Saurauia scabra may be attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and alkaloids, which can interact with pain receptors and reduce nociception. Additionally, the plant exhibits antifungal activities, particularly against pathogenic fungi, due to the presence of volatile oils and phenolic compounds.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Saurauia scabra are quercetin, kaempferol, phenolic acids, flavonoids, and saponins, which possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties due to their ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes. Quercetin and kaempferol, both flavonoids, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting NF-kappaB activation and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators. Phenolic acids, including ferulic and caffeic acids, have been found to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to their potential therapeutic applications. Flavonoids in Saurauia scabra have been reported to exhibit hepatoprotective, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer activities, while saponins have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Saurauia scabra may contribute to its traditional uses in folk medicine for various health conditions.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Saurauia scabra are infusion, tincture, tea, decoction, and salve. Infusion is a mixture of the plant's leaves in hot water to release its bioactive compounds, while tincture involves soaking the leaves in a solvent like ethanol to create a concentrated extract. Tea is prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water, similar to infusion, but often with a lower liquid-to-herb ratio. Decoction involves boiling the plant's roots or bark in water to release its active compounds, which is often used for Saurauia scabra's woodier parts. Salve is a topical preparation made by infusing the leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, to create a soothing and protective cream.

This family is further grouped within the order Ericales, which comprises several plant families known for their diverse range of species and characteristics. The family Actinidiaceae is native to tropical and subtropical regions, with many species being evergreen or semi-evergreen plants. The classification of Saurauia scabra also places it within the genus Saurauia, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in Southeast Asia. The specific classification of Saurauia scabra may also include further details at the species or infraspecific level.

intro: Saurauia scabra is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Actinidiaceae and commonly known as the "Tongkat Ali" or "Malaysian Ginseng" due to its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to enhance immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Saurauia scabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be found in various herbal supplements and capsules, as well as being used in the production of traditional remedies such as tonics and teas.
